title: eriophorum polystachion var. latifolium
intro: Eriophorum polystachion var. latifolium, commonly known as the broad-leaved cotton-grass, is a variant of the Eriophorum polystachion plant species, which is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the sedge family (Cyperaceae). The broad-leaved cotton-grass has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues, skin irritations, and wounds, as well as exhibiting antiviral and antimicrobial properties. Broad-leaved cotton-grass can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
